perturbation.original_text: Invoices will be paid within 15 days of receipt.
perturbation.changed_text: Invoices will be paid within 60 days of receipt.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a direct contradiction with Section 14, which implies services will continue only until the end of the month following notification, essentially requiring payment before the new 60-day invoice term. This introduces uncertainty about the actual payment deadline and what recourse TrueLink has if payment isn't received before the contract terminates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to Section 4 hereof, TrueLink will continue to provide the requested Hosting Services and Support Services until the last day of the month following the month in which Client provides TrueLink with a written notice of its election to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the invoice payment period from 15 days to 60 days. The contradicted text states that services will continue until the end of the month following the notice of termination. This means TrueLink may be required to provide services for up to a month after receiving a termination notice, while not being paid for those services until potentially 60 days after the invoice date. This directly contradicts the original understanding and creates a financial risk for TrueLink, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction, which falls under the definition of inconsistencies - in text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall renew automatically thereafter for successive one year periods until terminated pursuant to Section 12 herein or unless either Client or TrueLink deliver to the other written notice of intent not to renew no later than thirty (30) days prior to the end of said year.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall renew automatically thereafter for successive one year periods until terminated pursuant to Section 12 herein or unless either Client or TrueLink deliver to the other written notice of intent not to renew no later than fifteen (15) days prior to the end of said year.
perturbation.explanation: By shortening the notice period for non-renewal from 30 days to 15 days, this change creates a contradiction with Section 20, which specifies that notices are deemed given three days after mailing. This leaves only a 12-day window to send the notice by mail and still meet the new 15-day deadline, increasing the risk of disputes over timely notice and renewal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All notices will be deemed given (i) when delivered by hand, (ii) one (l) day after delivery to a reputable overnight carrier, or (iii) three (3) days after placement in first-class mail, postage prepaid, return receipt requested.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the notification period for non-renewal from 30 to 15 days. This directly conflicts with Section 20, which states notices are deemed given three days after mailing. The 15-day notification period in the changed text, combined with the three-day mailing provision in Section 20, creates a situation where timely notice may be impossible to achieve, creating an in-text contradiction because the deadline for mailing the notice now conflicts with the required notice period.

perturbation.original_text: TrueLink will be excused from delays in performing or from failing to perform its obligations under this Agreement to the extent the delays or failures result from causes beyond the reasonable control or TrueLink. However, to be excused from delay or failure to perform, TrueLink must act diligently to remedy the cause of the delay or failure.
perturbation.changed_text: TrueLink will be excused from delays in performing or from failing to perform its obligations under this Agreement to the extent the delays or failures result from causes beyond the control of TrueLink. TrueLink does not need to act diligently to remedy the cause of the delay or failure.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces a contradiction. Initially, TrueLink is excused for delays due to uncontrollable causes if they act diligently to fix it. The edited version removes the diligence requirement, conflicting with the original intent and potentially impacting Client's rights. This contradicts Section 11, which mandates a standard of care, because the revised Force Majeure clause implies a lower standard when uncontrollable events occur.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TrueLink shall perform the Hosting and Support Services and any other agreed-upon services for Client with the same degree of care, skill and prudence customarily exercised by it for its own operations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts Section 11 by removing the requirement for TrueLink to act diligently in remedying delays caused by uncontrollable events. Section 11 mandates that TrueLink perform services with a degree of care customarily exercised for its own operations. By eliminating the diligence requirement in the Force Majeure clause, the changed text implies a lower standard of care during uncontrollable events, directly conflicting with the standard of care mandated in Section 11. This inconsistency creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the required standard of care.
